document.title: Usability Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting intended use, usability test methodology, task results, conclusion, and referenced standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the usability engineering file by documenting summative usability validation activities, participant feedback, task completion rates, and conclusions on residual usability risk.
keyPoints: The ADD Abyz Digital Detector is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Seven participants with medical device education and a doctor or medical radiology technician equivalent licence took part in the usability test.
Nine usability tasks were evaluated and each task reported 100% completion with pass results.
The report concludes that usability goals met the acceptance criteria and residual usability risk is minimized and acceptable.
Referenced standards include EN ISO 14971:2007, EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015, and EN 62366:2008.

concepts.name: Intended use

concepts.evidence: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

concepts.name: Mammography exclusion

concepts.name: Usability test
concepts.evidence: A usability test is intended to determine the extent an interface facilitates a user’s ability to complete routine tasks.

concepts.name: Task completion success rate
concepts.evidence: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

concepts.name: Residual usability risk

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to obtain information necessary for patient diagnosis using anatomical structures from film or image processing during radiation exposure examination with cassette IP, CR, or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Abyz Digital Detector Intended Use

evidence.claim: ADD Abyz Digital Detector is a digital flat panel X-ray detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The usability test included seven participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants were involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Abyz Digital Detector Usability Validation

evidence.claim: Participants were persons educated about medical devices and having a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receive education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: The usability test goal for completion rate was 100%.
evidence.evidenceText: Completion Rate: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal

evidence.claim: The task completion success rate result was 100% pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: The evaluated usability tasks included user manual usability, power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, PC software settings, battery charging and change, patient arrangement, indicators, and cleaning.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1) Usability of User manual ACT #2) Connecting the power cord ACT #3) Cable code connection ACT #4) Main power button action ACT #5) PC software settings ACT #6) Battery charging, change ACT #7) Arrange the patient ACT #8) Indicators ACT #9) Cleaning
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Abyz Digital Detector Usability Tasks

evidence.claim: The overall data summary states that ADD usability was validated according to the usability verification and evaluation plan.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability of ADD have been validated according to the usability verification & evaluation plan.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Abyz Digital Detector Usability Validation

evidence.claim: The report states that some problems were carried out during usability testing and participant interviews and that proper risk control measures are required.
evidence.evidenceText: Based the usability verification & evaluation report, some problems are carried out during the usability testing, session of participants interview. Proper risk control measures are required to implement into the design to reduce the derived risks.

evidence.claim: The conclusion states that residual usability risk is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report states that no benefit outweigh risk evaluation was performed because usability goals met acceptance criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Since the usability verification & evaluation report demonstrates that the usability goal met the acceptance criteria set in the usability verification & evaluation plan, no evaluation of “benefit outweigh risk” is performed.

evidence.claim: Re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.
evidence.evidenceText: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2007 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2007 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report references EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 for usability collateral requirements for medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The report references EN 62366:2008 for application of usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is explicitly identified in the source text.
Specific product model identifiers are not stated in the source text, although the source metadata and path contain product signals.
Some acceptance criteria wording appears internally awkward, such as 'Are over 100% of operators', but task reports consistently state 100%.

Further Improvement states that problems occurred and risk controls are required, while the conclusion states residual usability risk is minimized and acceptable; details of the problems and actions are not included in the provided text.
